title: Digital Signage Content Delivery Health
app: Digital signage CMS / player agent via HEC
spl:
index=retail sourcetype="signage:health"
| eval sync_age_sec=now()-last_sync_epoch
| eval healthy=if(lower(download_status)="ok" AND sync_age_sec < 900, 1, 0)
| stats avg(healthy) as ok_frac by store_id, player_id
| eval health_pct=round(ok_frac*100,2)
| where health_pct < 95
| table store_id, player_id, health_pct
description: Failed content pulls leave blank or stale screens during campaigns; monitoring download success and player heartbeat protects brand and promotional compliance.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Digital Signage Content Delivery Health to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Standardize `download_status` across vendors. Alert when players miss sync for longer than the campaign refresh interval. Group by region for NOC-style triage. **Domain context:** Promotional compliance often requires proof-of-play; failed syncs can breach vendor agreements in franchise networks. **Splunk:** `now()-last_sync_epoch` must use the same clock as the player—prefer player-reported epoch to search `now()`.
visualization: Status grid (player × store), Single value (unhealthy %), Timeline (failed downloads).
knownFalsePositives: Signage health noise during content pushes, media-player firmware, firewall rule updates, or store-hour mismatches the signage team sees in the CMS.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Digital signage CMS / player agent via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=retail` `sourcetype="signage:health"` (store_id, player_id, content_id, download_status, last_sync_epoch).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Standardize `download_status` across vendors. Alert when players miss sync for longer than the campaign refresh interval. Group by region for NOC-style triage. **Domain context:** Promotional compliance often requires proof-of-play; failed syncs can breach vendor agreements in franchise networks. **Splunk:** `now()-last_sync_epoch` must use the same clock as the player—prefer player-reported epoch to search `now()`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=retail sourcetype="signage:health"
| eval sync_age_sec=now()-last_sync_epoch
| eval healthy=if(lower(download_status)="ok" AND sync_age_sec < 900, 1, 0)
| stats avg(healthy) as ok_frac by store_id, player_id
| eval health_pct=round(ok_frac*100,2)
| where health_pct < 95
| table store_id, player_id, health_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Digital Signage Content Delivery Health** — Failed content pulls leave blank or stale screens during campaigns; monitoring download success and player heartbeat protects brand and promotional compliance.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=retail` `sourcetype="signage:health"` (store_id, player_id, content_id, download_status, last_sync_epoch). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Digital signage CMS / player agent via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: retail; **sourcetype**: signage:health. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=retail, sourcetype="signage:health". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sync_age_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **healthy** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by store_id, player_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **health_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where health_pct < 95` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Digital Signage Content Delivery Health**): table store_id, player_id, health_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Reconcile amounts and counts with the POS journal or store close report for the same register and shift. Validate that returns, voids, and training transactions are tagged so they can be filtered if needed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (player × store), Single value (unhealthy %), Timeline (failed downloads).
grandmaExplanation: We watch signage players and content delivery. We help you catch black screens before campaigns and pricing fail in view.
